parsed_review.summary: CP00-804 (RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening, RP26.P05.M06.S09-RP26.P05.M06.S18, 10 units, risk A) passes independent read-only review as a descriptor-only, no-runtime, no-real-data pack with no runtime/enterprise-trust overclaim. Identity/coverage/deliverable distribution agree across registry binding, manifest, live plan, and contract; no-write attestation, per-row no-leak guards, authority boundary, Hermes/Claude packets, and contract promotion (CP00-804 latest, CP788-803 historical, validation.valid=true) all verified; local validation and readiness evidence are clean. One non-blocking P3: the four downstream closeout artifacts are not yet generated, which is expected at the pre-review stage and enforced by the closeout-pack:validate gate before commit. Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts not yet generated (expected at pre-review stage)
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts lists manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but Glob of docs/closeout-packs/cp00-804/ returns only manifest.json. This is consistent with a mid-closeout pack whose pack_level_claude_review.status is 'review_pending' with valid_review_receipts=[] and invalid_review_attempts=[] (manifest lines 217-225): claude-review-result.json and the review receipt are produced by/after this very review, while command-evidence.json (npm validation command outputs), adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are produced at closeout finalization. The immediately prior pack CP00-803 (already closed) contains all five artifacts and its review receipt recorded the identical pre-review condition as a single non-blocking P3, confirming this is sequencing rather than a defect. Because these are downstream of the review, the adjudication and construction-inspection inputs and the actual npm command-execution results named in the review focus cannot be independently verified from within this receipt; however the implementation (registry.js:324 binding/requirements, service.js:1186 descriptor + Hermes/Claude/handoff packets, validators.js:146 guards), tests (model.test.js:695-751), contract promotion (current_pack/latest_pack/latest_projection CP00-804 with validation.valid=true at contract line 30305), Hermes/Claude packets, local review-harness validation evidence (validation-report.json, readiness-report.json), and the no-write/no-real-data boundaries were all independently verified and pass, and the manifest does not yet claim production_ready=true (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false), so there is no overclaim to block.
findings.recommendation: No action required for this review. Ensure npm run closeout-pack:validate (which enforces required_pack_artifacts at lines 222-225, exactly-one valid_review_receipts matching review_receipt_ref with receipt.valid_review=true at lines 325-356, and the adjudication 'Production ready after adjudication: yes' marker at lines 402-410) plus the full validation command set (closeout-pack-plan:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane validators, git diff --check) are run and pass before the final CP00-804 closeout commit, so command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are present and adjudicated prior to flipping production_ready and committing.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected/non-blocking. Artifacts are generated by this review plus the closeout finalization step and enforced by the closeout-pack:validate gate before commit; does not block pack or goal closeout.
